Brilliant H214 / A75
Technical Data Sheet
__________________________________________________________________________________
Description
Brilliant H214 / A75 is a solvent based two component polyurethane laminating adhesive for flexible
packaging and industrial applications. Brilliant H214 / A75 is a high-performance adhesive and offers high green
and cured bond strength and optical clarity, as well as excellent heat and chemical resistance properties.
Brilliant H214 / A75 adheres to a wide range of printed and unprinted substrates including polyester,
polyethylene, polypropylene, aluminum foil, and metalized substrates.
Properties
Brilliant H214
OH Terminated
Brilliant A75
NCO Terminated
Solids 66% +/- 2% 75% +/- 2%
Viscosity 3,750 +/- 750 cPs
(Brookfield LVT Sp 3, 12 rpm/25°C)
14.5 +/- 1.0 seconds
(B-4 Ford Cup at 50% solids)
Density 1.10 +/- 0.02 g/cm3 1.08 +/- 0.02 g/cm3
Solvent Ethyl Acetate Ethyl Acetate
Colour Pale Yellow to Slight Brown Colourless to Pale Yellow
Appearance Transparent to Slight Hazy Transparent to Slight Hazy
Mix Ratio
H214 / A75 can be used at recommended mix ratio, depending on the performance requirement of the
laminate and processing / climatic conditions. A small trial before a bulk production run is always
recommended. (In case of necessity of change in ratio, for betterment in intended application, our TS team can
be contacted.)
Mix Ratio Brilliant H214
OH Terminated
Brilliant A75
NCO Terminated
For laminations involving metalized films or where a softer cure is required, we
recommend a mix ratio of 100 : 13 by weight 100 13
For film to film Laminations and PE based laminate structure where a harder cure
is desired, we recommend a mix ratio of 100 : 15 by weight 100 15
Uses
Brilliant H214 / A75 is suitable for manufacturing flexible packaging materials for snack foods, spices,
condiments & other food items, detergents, shampoos, soaps, other personal care items, liquor & packing of
aggressive products. It is well suited for hot fill as well as packing aggressive goods and other such high-
performance end uses. It offers excellent machineability and wettability on various substrates and can be used
to manufacture triplex laminates.
Dilution Table

Brilliant H214 / A75 has to be diluted with Ethyl Acetate to achieve the desired application solids. Ethyl
Acetate used must be of high purity with low residual alcohol and moisture content. See Dilution Chart below:

(B-4 Ford Cup

25% 100 15 194.0 15.0 – 18.0
30% 100 15 142.5 16.0 – 20.0
35% 100 15 105.7 22.0 – 25.0
Pot Life
Brilliant H214 / A75 mixed adhesive has a pot life of more than 8 hours when stored properly. Usage of
mixed adhesive beyond its pot life could cause lamination defects and is not recommended.
Recommended Operating Conditions
____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
1. The adhesive and hardener must be mixed and diluted in accordance with the dilution table above. Recommended
application solids range from 25% to 35% solids.
2. Please discuss your requirement of coating cylinder specifications with your cylinder supplier.
3. The dry adhesive deposit applied may range from 1.5 to 4.0 g/m². Laminates requiring higher heat and chemical
resistance will require higher dry adhesive deposit weights.
4. For printed films the application weight has to be adjusted accordingly and should be around 1 g/m² higher than
for unprinted films. Films should be printed with inks suitable for lamination.
5. All polyolefin films must be treated to a minimum level of 38 to 40 dynes / cm prior to lamination
6. Variables including line speed, drying tunnel temperatures, air flow rate and recirculation should be monitored
closely to ensure complete removal of all solvents.
7. Tension controls must also be precise and care must be taken to ensure that the tension of the substrates are
adjusted so that the stretch of the substrates is equal, thereby ensuring no curling or tunneling of the laminate.
8. Conditions at the laminating nip are extremely important to ensure healthy lamination. Adequate temperature
and pressure considering the laminate structure must be maintained.
9. Depending on climatic conditions, the bond-strength will be sufficient for further processing such as slitting and
rewinding in 24 hours.
10. Optimal heat and chemical resistance will develop within 5 - 10 days at ambient conditions.
11. Recommended cleaning solvents are Ethyl Acetate or other suitable organic solvents like MEK.
12. A special cleaning solvent, Brilliant C912, is available which can be used as cleaning agent. Please contact our Sales
Team for more information.
__________________________________________________________________________________
Safety, Handling & Storage
Brilliant H214 / A75 has a storage life of 12 months when stored indoors in a cool and dry, well-ventilated
area. Once opened, the product should be consumed within a short period, and care must be taken to re-seal

-- 2 of 3 --

Brilliant Polymers Pvt. Ltd.
Registered Office: 78 Jolly Maker Chambers II, Nariman Point, Mumbai 400021, India. P: +91 251 350 2181
Corporate Office and Factory: Plot 15,16,21/4, MIDC Morivali, Ambernath (West), Thane 421 505, India.
P: + 91 251 350 2000 E: info@brilliantpolymers.com www.brilliantpolymers.com
the containers properly. Drums must be stored away from heat and ignition sources.
Adequate safety measures should be followed before opening, dispensing, mixing, pouring or emptying
product from the drums. Operators must follow appropriate safety and hygiene guidelines while handling the
product. Containers can be hazardous when empty and must be disposed off in accordance with local
regulations. Safety Data Sheets are available for all Brilliant Polymers products. Refer to SDS of Brilliant H214
and Brilliant A75 for more information on all safety aspects.
FDA Compliance / Food Safety Compliance
Brilliant H214 / A75 is in compositional compliance with US FDA 21 CFR 175.105 for Laminating Adhesives.
Under specific conditions of the FDA, EC and BfR regulations, the adhesive Brilliant H214 / A75 is suitable for
manufacture of packaging materials for packing of food.
Brilliant H214 / A75 contains aromatic isocyanates. We therefore recommend migration testing of the fully cured
material under conditions of use, particularly with high temperature applications, to demonstrate compliance
with the limits for primary aromatic amines (PAA). It must be noted that adhesive must be allowed to cure
completely before such testing is carried out.
Further, exact end use conditions like substrates, inks and other additives, and end product to be packed in the
packaging material are unknown to us. Hence, responsibility for compliance is with the converter and not with
the adhesive supplier. This includes regulatory as well as end application testing.
The adhesive supplier’s responsibility is to supply products within the specifications in the TDS and COA along
with comprehensive food safety statements.
Disclaimer & Other Information
-The Information provided in this data sheet, especially recommendations for the usage and the application of our products,
is based on our knowledge and experience. Due to varying working conditions beyond our control, we strictly recommend
comprehensive trials to test the suitability of our products with regard to the required end-use application.
-Laminating conditions like temperatures and pressure, application method and adhesive deposit, films and substrates
used, and film additives like slip additive, packed goods, printing inks, pre-treatment and coating of films are significant to
the performance properties of the laminated packaging materials. Film additives - particularly slip agent content in LDPE,
MDPE and CPP films - packed goods, printing inks, pre-treatment and coating of films are of significant importance to the
intended use of the laminates and may affect the performance properties either straight away or later. Therefore, practical
laminating tests as well as checks on the laminates are necessary before going into bulk production.
-We strongly recommend convertors to take trial runs and establish stability studies for same. Once such stability
established structure is commercialised, any further changes in components like inks/substrate properties needs to be
revalidated. For any impacts due to changes, adhesive supplier will not be responsible and will not hold any liability in such
cases.
Information provided here is based on our knowledge of our products, especially recommendations of usage and
application. Based on working conditions and structures it needs to be necessarily evaluated and established by convertor,
by its own. We do not accept any liability with regard to the above information.
This datasheet replaces all former versions and recommendations.
Date of Release: May 2025
